summary: Kenneth demonstrates his weekly covered call strategy, showing how he deployed $64,881 across multiple positions to generate $2,600 (4%) in premium income in half an hour. He walks through the mechanics of buying 100 shares and selling weekly covered calls using Charles Schwab's Think or Swim platform, using Snapchat ($8.40/share) as a low-cost example.
takeaways: Sell covered calls on weekly expiration for recurring income
Minimum 100 shares required per covered call contract (1 contract = 100 shares)
Find stock candidates using CNBC market movers filtered by volatility, unusual volume, or most active
Use bid column in Think or Swim to sell covered calls at bid price
Calculate percentage gain on capital deployed: premium received ÷ stock cost × 100 (e.g., $19 premium on $839 cost = 2.26%)
Target 4% return weekly through multiple simultaneous positions ($64,881 deployed for $2,600 premium)
Lower-priced stocks like Snapchat ($840 total for 100 shares) are more accessible than expensive stocks ($23,000+)

strategy_candidates.change: Systematize stock selection by filtering CNBC movers for volatility + unusual volume metrics to improve fill consistency across the $50k Premium Desk
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth explicitly uses CNBC market movers (top/bottom, most active, unusual volume) as primary method to identify candidates for covered call writing each week

strategy_candidates.change: Target 2-4% weekly return per position (matching Kenneth's 2.26% Snapchat example and $2,600 on $64,881 total, roughly 4%) as benchmark for premium collection threshold
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates 2.26% on individual trade and 4% overall weekly return ($2,600/$64,881), suggesting this is his expected range for position sizing

title: I just spent $64,881 - what did I buy??
